prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response: Waking up is a consistently underwhelming product with poor user interface and frequent performance issues. The abrupt transition from sleep to consciousness lacks intuitive design, often accompanied by jarring alarms, disorientation, and immediate system errors like grogginess or dry mouth. Battery life (i.e., energy levels) is rarely sufficient for the advertised full-day runtime, and the mandatory daily update cycle offers no opt-out feature. While occasional firmware glitches (e.g., oversleeping) provide brief relief, the overall experience remains non-negotiable, non-refundable, and—despite decades of user feedback—stubbornly unchanged. Not recommended, but unfortunately non-optional.
